lede: A look at March 2 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ricardo Lagos and Marcel Hirscher, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Oscar selfie
events.desc: The Oscar Selfie, regarded as one of the most influential and important images of all time, is taken at the 86th Academy Awards.

events.title: Fermi National Accelerator Laboratory
events.desc: Researchers at Fermilab announce the discovery of the top quark.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: First German military units enter Bulgaria after it joins the Axis Pact.

events.title: Steel Workers Organizing Committee
events.desc: The Steel Workers Organizing Committee signs a collective bargaining agreement with U.S. Steel, leading to unionization of the United States steel industry.

events.title: U.S. Steel
events.desc: United States Steel Corporation is founded as a result of a merger between Carnegie Steel Company and Federal Steel Company which became the first corporation in the world with a market capital over $1 billion.

events.title: Great Slave Auction
events.desc: The two-day Great Slave Auction, once thought to be the largest such auction in United States history, begins.

events.title: Texas Revolution
events.desc: Texas Revolution: The Declaration of independence of the Republic of Texas from Mexico is adopted.

events.title: Claude Chappe
events.desc: Claude Chappe demonstrates the first semaphore line near Paris.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: Patriot militia units attempt to prevent capture of supply ships in and around the Savannah River by a small fleet of the Royal Navy in the Battle of the Rice Boats.

events.title: College of Arms
events.desc: The College of Arms is formally incorporated by Royal Charter signed by King Richard III of England.

births.name: Ricardo Lagos
births.line: Chilean economist, lawyer, and politician, 33rd President of Chile

births.name: Pope Pius XII
births.line: Head of the Catholic Church from 1939 to 1958

births.name: Mikhail Gorbachev
births.line: Russian lawyer and politician, the 8th and final leader of the Soviet Union, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Pope Leo XIII
births.line: Head of the Catholic Church from 1878 to 1903

deaths.text: João Bernardo Vieira, Bissau-Guinean politician, President of Guinea-Bissau
Serge Gainsbourg, French singer-songwriter, actor, and director
D. H. Lawrence, English novelist, poet, playwright, and critic
Heinrich Wilhelm Matthias Olbers, German physician and astronomer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Angela of the Cross
Christian feast day: Blessed Charles the Good, Count of Flanders
Christian feast day: Chad of Mercia (Catholic Church, Anglican Communion, Eastern Orthodox Church)
